content.text: Claude's Enterprise plan is built for organisations operating under regulatory frameworks — healthcare (HIPAA), EU operations (GDPR), information security programmes (ISO 27001), and broad vendor due diligence (SOC 2). This document explains which certifications Anthropic holds, what data handling commitments apply by default, and what your organisation must configure to meet each framework's requirements.

Certifications and compliance posture change over time. Always confirm the current status with your Anthropic account team and request the latest SOC 2 report, ISO certificate, and DPA or BAA before signing a contract that names Claude as a sub-processor.

attrs.alt: Claude Enterprise compliance and certification landscape

content.text: SOC 2 Type II is an AICPA audit assessing a service provider's controls over a 6-12 month period. Anthropic maintains a SOC 2 Type II report covering the
trust service criteria. The report is updated annually and shared with Enterprise customers under NDA.
Using the SOC 2 report in vendor due diligence
— covers the most recent 12 months; older reports are weaker evidence.
Read the Complementary User Entity Controls (CUECs)
— obligations you must satisfy for the controls to function as described (e.g., manage your API keys, enforce MFA).
Map CUECs to your control framework
and document how you satisfy each one for your own auditor.
SOC 2 Type II covers the audit period, not the present moment. Subscribe to Anthropic's status page for real-time assurance between report cycles.

attrs.alt: HIPAA-compliant deployment required controls and responsibilities

content.text: using Claude. Anthropic qualifies as a Business Associate under HIPAA, so a signed
is required before any PHI is sent to the API.
Contact your Anthropic account team and request the standard Enterprise BAA.
Review the BAA with your legal team, confirming coverage of sub-processors.
Sign before go-live. Do NOT send PHI to the API before the BAA is countersigned.
Store the executed BAA in your vendor contract system with an annual review date.
Zero Data Retention (ZDR) for HIPAA workloads
With ZDR enabled, Anthropic does not store prompt or response content after the API call returns. This eliminates the risk of PHI persisting in Anthropic's systems beyond the transaction.

Console > Organization Settings > Data Privacy > Zero Data Retention: ON

Effect:
  Prompt content:    not stored after response is returned
  Response content:  not stored
  Metadata:          retained for billing (token counts, timestamps)
  Audit log events:  retained (no prompt content included)
ZDR does not cover your side:
If your application logs the full prompt or response for debugging, you are storing PHI — apply your own HIPAA safeguards (encryption, access controls, retention limits) to those logs.
HIPAA requires you to share only PHI necessary for the specific task. Design prompts to exclude identifiers when not required:

# Avoids unnecessary identifiers — anonymise when task allows
prompt = (
    "A 39-year-old male presents with acute chest pain radiating to the left arm, "
    "onset 2 hours ago. No prior cardiac history. "
    "What are the differential diagnoses?"
)

# Include identifiers only when the task specifically requires them
# and only after confirming BAA and ZDR are in place
GDPR applies when you process personal data of EU/EEA residents. Anthropic is a

must be in place before sending personal data to the API. Anthropic's standard DPA covers lawful basis, sub-processor disclosure, data subject rights support, security measures, and breach notification timelines.
EU org processing EU personal data
EU data processed on US infrastructure

With ZDR enabled, prompt/response data is not stored — erasure requests for API interactions are automatically satisfied. Without ZDR, contact Anthropic support for deletion within the retention window.
API call metadata is accessible via the usage endpoint; prompt/response content is only available within the default 30-day retention window (when ZDR is off).
Exclude personal identifiers from prompts unless strictly required — same principle as HIPAA minimum necessary.
ISO 27001 certifies that Anthropic operates an

covering risk management, asset management, access control, cryptography, supplier relationships, and incident management. The certificate is issued by an accredited body and is typically valid for 3 years with annual surveillance audits.
For your own ISO 27001 programme, Anthropic's certification satisfies the
control (Annex A 5.19–5.22). Document Anthropic in your supplier register, reference the ISO certificate as evidence, and schedule an annual review to verify the certificate remains valid.
Enterprise audit logs stream as NDJSON and should be ingested into your SIEM within minutes — Anthropic's retention window is limited, so forward events immediately.
HIPAA requires 6-year retention; SOC 2 evidence typically covers 12 months. Forward all events to your own SIEM immediately — do not rely on Anthropic's retention window as your compliance record.
Yes — under NDA from account team
Yes — standard DPA; SCCs addendum on request
No — not by default; ZDR eliminates any retention
TLS 1.2+ in transit; AES-256 at rest
Compliance with HIPAA, GDPR, and ISO 27001 when using Claude follows a shared responsibility model — Anthropic controls the platform layer, you control the application and configuration layers.
Enable ZDR; verify settings match policy
Configure SSO; enforce MFA at IdP
Assign roles correctly; run access reviews
Prompt design, output filtering, app-level logging
Map controls, document CUECs, manage contracts
Adding Claude to your risk register
Your information security programme needs to track Claude as a critical third-party supplier. The risk register entry should include:
Claude AI API and claude.ai platform
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA available, GDPR DPA available
Annual — align with SOC 2 report refresh
BAA number and expiry / DPA version
CISO or designated AI governance lead
Request updated SOC 2 Type II report from account team.
Verify ISO 27001 certificate is still valid and in-scope.
Review any changes to Anthropic's sub-processor list (in DPA appendix).
Confirm ZDR, EU residency, and SSO settings still match current policy.
Run an access review: verify member roles, remove stale accounts.
Review audit log samples for unexpected privilege changes or access patterns.
Update the risk register with findings and the next review date.
The following settings, applied in order, put a new Claude Enterprise organisation into a compliance-ready state:

Step 1 — Execute legal agreements
  [ ] BAA signed (if processing PHI)
  [ ] DPA signed (if processing EU personal data)
  [ ] SCCs addendum added if EU data processed on US infra

Step 2 — Console configuration
  [ ] SSO enabled + domain verified
  [ ] MFA enforced at IdP
  [ ] Zero Data Retention: ON (HIPAA / high-sensitivity GDPR)
  [ ] EU data residency: ON (if required)
  [ ] SCIM provisioning: configured
  [ ] Audit log export: connected to SIEM

Step 3 — Admin API setup
  [ ] Workspace token limits set per team budget
  [ ] Workspace-scoped API keys created (not org-wide keys)
  [ ] Budget alerts active (80% / 95%)
  [ ] Key rotation schedule documented (90 days)

Step 4 — Application layer
  [ ] Prompts designed with minimum-necessary PHI principle
  [ ] Application-layer logs encrypted and access-controlled
  [ ] Output filtering for sensitive data categories
  [ ] Rate-limit backoff implemented in all API clients
When a security event involves Claude (e.g., an API key is compromised, or you suspect unauthorised access to Claude conversations), coordinate the response across both your team and Anthropic:
Revoke the compromised API key via Admin API.
Pull recent audit logs for the time window in question. Look for
Contact security@anthropic.com or your account team. Under the BAA/DPA, Anthropic has notification obligations for breaches on their side; you have obligations for breaches on yours.
Capture timeline, scope, affected data types, and remediation steps. This documentation is required for HIPAA breach assessment and GDPR Art. 33/34 notifications.
After containment, conduct a root cause analysis and update your controls (e.g., tighter key rotation, additional monitoring rules).

import anthropic, os
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_ADMIN_KEY"])

def incident_response(compromised_key_id: str, incident_start_iso: str):
    # 1. Revoke compromised key
    client.admin.api_keys.delete(api_key_id=compromised_key_id)
    print(f"Revoked key: {compromised_key_id}")

    # 2. Pull audit logs from incident window
    events = []
    after = None
    while True:
        page = client.admin.audit_logs.list(
            limit=100,
            start_time=incident_start_iso,
            **({'after': after} if after else {})
        )
        events.extend(page.data)
        if not page.has_more:
            break
        after = page.last_id

    # 3. Filter to events involving the compromised key
    related = [e for e in events
               if getattr(e, 'api_key_id', None) == compromised_key_id]
    print(f"Found {len(related)} events tied to compromised key")
    for e in related:
        print(f"  {e.event_time} {e.event_type}")

incident_response("apikey_01compromised", "2026-07-01T00:00:00Z")

Claude Enterprise is backed by SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA BAA, GDPR DPA, and ISO 27001. Your shared responsibility includes: executing the BAA or DPA before processing regulated data, enabling Zero Data Retention for HIPAA and high-sensitivity GDPR workloads, activating EU data residency if required, forwarding audit logs to your SIEM with long-term retention, and documenting Anthropic in your supplier register with annual certificate reviews. Anthropic secures the platform; your organisation secures its configuration, access controls, and application-layer data handling.
